description: High scores on OECD Better Life Work-Life Balance index. Low share of workers working very long hours (>50h/week). High reported life satisfaction.
model_validation_target: Simulated Type A avg_stress should be LOW (<0.25) and avg available_time should remain HIGH (>3h/day) at equilibrium.
source_note: Stylised from OECD Better Life Index 2023 data for economies with strong autonomy norms and high-quality social services. Scores normalised to [0,10]. No specific country named.

description: Middle-range work-life balance. Moderate share of long-hours workers.
model_validation_target: Simulated mid-range preset avg_stress ~0.35-0.45; avg available_time ~2-3h/day.
source_note: Stylised from OECD Better Life Index 2023 mid-quartile economies.

description: Lower work-life balance scores despite high per-capita GDP growth. High share of workers working very long hours. Satisfaction maintained through consumption convenience but time-scarcity is structural.
model_validation_target: Simulated Type B avg_stress should be HIGH (>0.50) and avg available_time LOW (<1.5h/day) at equilibrium after involution.
source_note: Stylised from OECD and non-OECD high-growth economies with extensive service sectors. No specific country named.
